body: Per-step execution capabilities + **global status auditability** (user, evaluating the model against `web/canonical.html`). The canonical WASC action-step grid needs three per-`ActionStep` capabilities the model lacked (source-model-gaps §B): **assessment**, **per-step communications**, **status**. User decided per-step status is **accreditation-defensible** → the **audited** form, and endorsed auditability **globally** (accreditation + school-level accountability). Implemented in three slices: (A) `ActionStep.assessment` (translated TextField) + `ActionStep.communications` M2M (`deca4d5`, plans/0025); (B) audited `ActionStep.status` — new reusable `StatusTransition` abstract base + `ActionStepStatusTransition` append-only log + `set_action_step_status` service + readonly status + bulk "Mark …" actions + view-only school-scoped transition admin (`cba276f`, plans/0026–0027); (C) audited `Evidence.status` — `EvidenceStatusTransition` (reuses the base) + `set_evidence_status` + readonly + actions + view-only admin, closing source-model-gaps §H's audit half (`c54f8046`, plans/0028–0029). This completes status-auditability across the system (Plan lifecycle already logged; Milestone reached already stamped; now ActionStep + Evidence). Decided **out**: a `blocked` status token (mockup-only, not in US-ORG-18) and a transition `note` field (not in any source/US). Decided **separate/out of scope**: §H's Evidence artifact payload/location half. Gate green (pytest 607); applied to dev DB + verified (audit tables; view-only `view_*statustransition` to all four groups, no mutation grants). Enables US-ORG-18 (status, audited) + the data layer for US-ORG-19 (rollup view downstream).

body: "Blocked" action steps = **stored, first-class, queryable state** (user; corrects an initial misread toward "derived"). A user explicitly records a **blocker (impediment)** on an action step; the rationale is that a user must be able to **list all currently-blocked steps** directly, with *why / since when / who* (consistent with the audit-accountability stance of DEC-16 — a blocker is an accountability event). It is NOT a `status` value (blocked is orthogonal to the not-started/in-progress/done progress axis and coexists with in-progress — folding it into the enum would break the total order + rollup weights), NOT a bare boolean (which loses who/when/why and history), and NOT a dependency-derived read-side signal (the derived variant was the misread, rejected). Adopted as **US-ORG-20**. **IMPLEMENTED `cc79349`** (+ perm fix `3694fad`): `plans.ActionStepImpediment` (translated `reason`; server-stamped `raised_by`/`raised_at` + `resolved_by`/`resolved_at`; paired-resolution `CheckConstraint`) + `raise_impediment`/`resolve_impediment` services + school-scoped editable admin (resolve action, readonly stamps, open/resolved changelist filter) + `ImpedimentInline` on `ActionStepAdmin`; migrations plans/0030 (CreateModel) + plans/0031 (grant plan_author full, curator/read_only view) + plans/0032 (grant school_admin full — see below). Gate green (pytest 632); applied + verified on dev DB (table + all four group grants). US-ORG-20 → enabled.

body: `web/canonical.html` is a **required output materialization**, not a prototype to be aligned to the model. It is the formal WASC schoolwide action-plan document for a fixed audience (the visiting committee / accreditors) in a fixed shape (the WASC sample template). The model is the single source of truth; it must be rich enough to **materialize canonical** (a report/export view emits that exact shape from stored plan data) **and** to drive the staff-facing creation/visualization views — direction is model → rendering. Implications: (1) canonical keeps its WASC-**audience** vocabulary (e.g. the header "Department / Person(s) Responsible"; KPI chips rendered from a target-kind `SuccessCriterion`) — it is **not** relabeled to db field names the way the staff mockups are; (2) every datum canonical shows must be materializable — where it is not, the **model** changes (a gap recorded in `data/source-model-gaps.md`, mandate-007), canonical is never edited down. DEC-16 (the 2-of-6 WASC action-step columns `assessment`/`communications` + audited `status`) and DEC-17 (`ActionStepImpediment`) were already instances of this. **`web/working.html` + `web/wizard.html` are staff creation/visualization views → SAME treatment as `mockups/a–e`** (rendered language + capabilities aligned to db terminology; user direction 2026-05-23). `web/data/plan-data.js` is the shared sample/import source (canonical reads it too); its existing prototype keys are **not** renamed (the import-source role + canonical's distinct treatment both depend on it). Recorded as the "Materialization principle" framing at the head of `data/source-model-gaps.md`.

body: Resolves the data↔view split surfaced by aligning `web/working.html`/`web/wizard.html` (their two STOP-flagged structural mismatches: improvement-type **altitude** [per-`Milestone` in the model; the prototypes have no milestone tier] and **Role-encoded actors** [Role retired; model uses `division` + optional `person`]). User decision (think-out 2026-05-23): **`web/data/plan-data.js` is to be made EXACTLY faithful to the Django modeling** — it is the JS stand-in for *stored plan data*, shaped like the models (`divisions` with `parent`/`head`; the `Milestone` tier `Plan → Milestone → Phase → ActionStep` with per-milestone `improvement_type`/`planning_method`; actors `division` + optional `person`; per-step `status`/`assessment`/`communications`/`impediments`; quantification as a target-kind `SuccessCriterion`, no standalone KPI). Single model-faithful sample source of truth. **`web/canonical.html` stays as it is as a VIEW**: rendered WASC output/shape held constant; it does NOT bring in elements its shape doesn't display (it projects the model-faithful data *down* to the fixed WASC document, omitting the milestone tier / division hierarchy it doesn't surface). Operative reading: "stays as it is" = **output-frozen**, not byte-frozen — its renderer is updated to read the model-faithful keys while preserving output, guarded by a **golden-output check** against the WASC sample (`sources/`). The "original prototype" divergence record in `data/source-model-gaps.md` is pinned to the prototype's **git SHA** (git freezes prior state); `plan-data.js` evolves past it. `web/working.html`/`web/wizard.html` read the same model-faithful data and show milestones/divisions natively. Builds on DEC-18. **IMPLEMENTED** (2026-05-23): plan-data.js rebuilt model-faithful + canonical.html re-pointed output-frozen (`cf09618`; golden contract `web/data/canonical-golden.json`, zero shape diff); working.html + wizard.html switched to native structural keys (`ca8ad5c`; wizard gained a Milestone step at the improvement_type/planning_method altitude); prototype pinned at `529b3d0` in `data/source-model-gaps.md`. Persisted at the head of `data/source-model-gaps.md`.
continuation: - **Plumbing needed (a new model + service + admin + perms + one migration):**

1. **Model** `plans.ActionStepImpediment` (per-`ActionStep` child, atomic-work altitude): `action_step` FK→ActionStep CASCADE `related_name="impediments"`; `reason` (translated `TextField`, EN/中文, like `assessment`/`description`); `raised_by` FK→User PROTECT + `raised_at` `DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)`; `resolved_by` FK→User PROTECT null/blank + `resolved_at` `DateField`/`DateTimeField` null/blank. A **CheckConstraint** pairing the resolution fields (both null = open, both set = resolved) — the `Q`-disjunction idiom of `Milestone.milestone_reached_fields_match_status`. "Open"/"blocked" = `resolved_at__isnull=True`.

2. **Service** (`plans/services.py`, server-stamped who/when, atomic): `raise_impediment(step, reason, by_user)` (create open impediment, stamp `raised_by`); `resolve_impediment(impediment, by_user)` (stamp `resolved_by`/`resolved_at`). Mirrors `mark_milestone_reached` — stamps are server-written, not hand-edited.

3. **Admin** (`plans/admin/`): `ActionStepImpedimentAdmin` with `SchoolScopedAdminMixin`, `school_lookup="action_step__plan__school"`; `reason` editable; `raised_by`/`raised_at`/`resolved_by`/`resolved_at` **readonly** (server-stamped); a "Resolve selected" action (stamps `request.user`); raise = create (stamp `raised_by=request.user` in `save_model`). "List all blocked" = a changelist filter on `resolved_at__isnull`. (Editable child — NOT a view-only audit log, so **DISC-19's `school_admin` view-only strip does NOT apply**; `school_admin` correctly keeps full CRUD via `school/0036`.)

4. **Perms** (DISC-03 plans group-extension migrations): grant `plan_author` full CRUD, `school_curator` + `read_only` view-only (plans/0031), and **`school_admin` full CRUD explicitly (plans/0032)**. (Originally 0031 relied on `school/0036`'s create-permissions-on-live-registry to grant `school_admin` — but that only fires on a FRESH build; on a DB where 0036 already ran before this model existed [the dev DB] `school_admin` got nothing, so plans/0032 grants it explicitly. Refines DISC-19: never rely on 0036 for already-migrated DBs — every new plans model sets `school_admin`'s grant in its own migration.)

5. **Migration:** `plans` `CreateModel ActionStepImpediment` (+ its translated `reason` columns) — additive, SQLite-safe (new table; the paired-resolution CheckConstraint is on the new model, no replay on existing tables) — plus the group-extension RunPython. No change to `ActionStep` or `status`.

- **Orthogonal note (separate, not part of DEC-17):** dependency-driven "implicitly blocked by an unfinished predecessor" (`ActionStepDependency` + predecessor `status`) remains a possible *derived* convenience signal, but it is NOT what DEC-17 builds — DEC-17 is the explicit, user-recorded, stored impediment.

body: Post-model architecture orientation (user think-out: "what other canonical Django-universe elements to use, given the AI-assisted wizard plan-creation vision"; the zero-API/zero-views posture so far was a deliberate FIRST FOCUS to concentrate on data modeling, not a permanent exclusion). Captured WITHOUT prejudicing any direction.
continuation: - **Settled principle (an invariant, not a direction):** the **model is the contract**. Every layer that writes plan data — Django views, forms/formsets, the create-plan wizard, AND any AI author-assist — goes through the SAME model invariants (`CheckConstraint`/`clean()`) and the `services.py` domain operations. The AI gets **no privileged write path**: it proposes data that the same constraints validate, and the seeded reference vocabularies (improvement_types, planning_methods+steps, AreasForImprovement, accreditation standards, learner outcomes) are simultaneously its grounding/context and its guardrails. This is why model-first was the right first focus.

- **Captured landscape (REFERENCE only — not a selection):** (a) *request/response layer*, deferred on purpose — views (CBV/FBV), URLs, templates, `ModelForm` + inline formsets (the create-plan spine; US-UI-1's structured timeline is a form-widget/formset concern), a multi-step wizard (`django-formtools` `SessionWizardView` or HTMX-driven CBVs); draft state is already the model's `lifecycle="proposed"` + the `advance_plan_status` promotion gate. (b) *AI-assist-specific* — background tasks (Django 6.0 `django.tasks` interface, or Celery/`django-q`) to move the slow/external/failable LLM call off the request cycle; async views + `StreamingHttpResponse`/SSE to stream suggestions; an `ai` service module extending the `services.py` pattern (mockable, keeps the gate fast); caching; env-held API key (Layer-B pattern). (c) *cross-cutting* — management commands (US-ONB-4 CSV import; AI batch jobs), `FileField`/Storage API (the open `source-model-gaps §H` Evidence-payload gap), `messages` + permission mixins wiring the existing groups into views. (d) *deliberately keep out, per standing stances* — signals for domain logic (explicit services preferred; no hooks), a full DRF/REST API or heavy SPA unless a consumer appears (the `web/drafts/architect-review` conclusion: "model is clean enough to add DRF later; do not pre-build"), 2FA (removed `80f46ae`).

- **Explicitly UNCHOSEN (no prejudice):** the front-end direction (the five `mockups/` remain the decision input); every tech choice above (CBV vs `formtools` wizard vs HTMX; built-in `django.tasks` vs Celery; SSE vs Channels; DRF vs none; whether to build the AI assist at all) is OPEN.

- **Flagged (a consideration, not a decision):** the AI call's slowness forces work off the request cycle, so the background-task + async-streaming path is the one element that shapes settings + deployment (a worker process) and the wizard's interaction model — worth deciding early when a direction is taken. No code, no commitment this turn. Persisted in the spine (LOG + STATE).

body: Reuse the LLM service elements from **`django-llm-template`** (`gh:davidorex/django-llm-template`; local `/Users/david/Projects/digitalocean/django-llm-template/template/core/`) for this project's AI author-assist. Same-author Copier template; assessed a strong fit as a **harvest source** (NOT whole-template adoption — this project already exists and deliberately diverged on auth [custom onboarding] and API [no DRF]). **Likely the next build; the audit/config/template elements land as Django models here ("in the models").** **Decoupled from Celery/Redis** — the service layer is infrastructure-independent; per the celery/redis discussion the AI-wizard's natural path is streaming (async view + SSE, no extra infra), and a background worker is a separate later choice (and if ever needed, Django 6.0 `django.tasks` on Postgres avoids Redis). This narrows DEC-20's AI-service piece (source chosen) WITHOUT prejudicing the rest (front-end direction; Celery-vs-`django.tasks`; whether/when the wizard ships).
continuation: - **Reuse inventory:**

1. **LLM service layer** — `core/services/llm/` (`__init__.py`: `get_llm()` / `get_llm_logged()`; `base.py`: `LLMProvider` / `LLMResponse`; `providers/`: anthropic, openai, openrouter, deepseek, openai_compat). Provider-abstracted `.complete()` + `.stream()`; uses `httpx` (no heavy SDK). → a `services/llm` (or `ai`) package here.

2. **Models (the "in the models" part)** — from `core/models/llm.py`: **`LLMProviderConfig`** (admin-editable runtime provider/key/default-model; system or per-user), **`PromptTemplate`** (reusable, variable substitution, sanitizes on render), **`LLMCall`** (audit log of every call — provider/model/usage/status/timestamps; fits this project's audit-everything culture). → Django models here, adapted to project conventions.

3. **Prompt sanitizer** — `core/services/prompt_sanitizer.py` (OWASP LLM01 prompt-injection defense; `sanitize_data` for user data, `sanitize_refinement` for user instructions). High-value security; take it.
4. **Settings shape** — `LLM_API_KEYS` / `LLM_DEFAULT_PROVIDER` / `LLM_DEFAULT_MODEL` / `LLM_REQUEST_TIMEOUT`, env-driven (Layer-B pattern); template default is Anthropic `claude-sonnet-4.5`.

5. **Optional / deferred (not core):** the SSE streaming utility (take when the wizard streams — async view + SSE, no infra) and the Celery `shared_task` pattern (only if a background worker is later chosen; else `django.tasks`).
- **NOT reused:** django-allauth (conflicts with the built onboarding US-ONB-*), the DRF stack (no-API posture), crispy-forms, the whole Copier scaffold.

- **Adaptation notes for the build:** place in a dedicated app (`ai`/`llm`) or under an existing app; fit this project's base-model / `Meta` defaults; the LLM infra models are operational English-only (no `modeltranslation`); decide multi-school tenancy on `LLMCall` / `PromptTemplate` (carry a `school` / `plan` FK or keep system-scoped); admin + perms likely app-admin/superuser only (NOT `school_admin`); wire `PromptTemplate` grounding to the seeded reference vocabularies (DEC-20 — the seeded improvement_types / planning_methods / AFIs / standards / learner outcomes are the AI's grounding + guardrails). ~~Not yet built — own plan→impl.~~ **IMPLEMENTED `108e90f`** (the LLM service slice): new `ai` app — `TimestampedModel` base; `LLMProviderConfig` / `PromptTemplate` (sanitizer-on-render) / `LLMCall` (audit) models; `ai.services.llm` (`get_llm` / `get_llm_logged` / `LoggedLLMProvider` + provider registry over httpx: anthropic [default] / openai / openrouter / deepseek / openai_compat); `ai.services.prompt_sanitizer` (OWASP LLM01); plain-`ModelAdmin` superuser-only admin (LLMCall read-only); `LLM_*` env settings (default Anthropic); `httpx>=0.27` added. Gate green (check 0, makemigrations clean, ruff `ai/` clean [repo baseline unchanged: 6 pre-existing in school/0027 + test_seed_positions], **mypy 221**, **pytest 686** [+54]). `ai/0001_initial` applied to dev DB; verified the 3 `ai_*` tables + 12 perms exist and **no group (incl. `school_admin`) holds any `ai` perm** — superuser-only by construction (the new app is outside `school/0036`'s 4-app sweep, so the DISC-19 trap does not fire; no group-extension migration needed). NOT built (later slices): views/wizard, Celery/SSE, AI-prompt/grounding logic, and a `school`/`plan` tenancy FK on `LLMCall` (additive when the wizard wires AI calls to a plan). Known SQL property (documented in tests): the `unique_provider_per_user` / `unique_default_per_user` constraints do not block duplicate *system-wide* (`user=NULL`) rows — NULLs are distinct in SQL; they fire only for a set user.

body: Front-end conventions precedent established + applied (user: "best practices built into mocks such that rendering decided upon into the django universe carries already made best-practices decisions"). New doc **`FRONTEND-CONVENTIONS.md`** (governs `mockups/` + `web/`, NOT `web/canonical.html` [output-frozen] nor `web/data/plan-data.js` [shared classic data source]). Best-of-breed 2026, no build step: **native ES modules** (one `<script type="module">` `main.js` entry + responsibility-split submodules: data / i18n / format / render / events) and **CSS partials** (`tokens.css` → `layout.css` → `components.css`, cascade-ordered via multiple `<link>`s — no `@import`); disk layout approaching Django **templates/static** (`mockups/<x>/{js,css}/`; `web/static/{js,css}/<page>/`); **no god files**; ESM needs an http preview (`python3 -m http.server`, noted per page). Invariants: identical render/behavior, EN/简体中文 toggle preserved, no frameworks/CDNs, no new `window` globals (web pages may only READ the shared `plan-data.js` globals). **Applied** (`6d65ae4`) across all five `mockups/a–e` + `web/working.html` + `web/wizard.html` in two folded passes: (1) extraction of remaining inline `<style>`/`<script>` to files (+ normalized d-dashboard's flat files into `css/`+`js/`); (2) modularization of every god JS file into ES modules + every god stylesheet into the three partials (one agent per surface). web pages keep `plan-data.js` classic + loaded first; their modules read `window.DATA`/`REF`/`PlanHelpers`. d-dashboard verified by headless-Chrome rendered-`<body>` diff across all 10 lang×role combos → byte-identical; all JS modules pass `node --check`. **Naming-convention standardization across all mockup+web CSS/JS is the in-progress follow-on** (audit + normalize identifiers/classes/tokens/files to one standard, zero render change, for Django-readiness incl. possibly 2–3 template suites during dev). **DONE `bc86b57`** (one agent): CSS custom properties harmonized to `--<category>-<name>` across all 7 surfaces (~600+ `var()` sites); d-dashboard ids → kebab; e-wizard i18n keys → snake_case; documented the standard (incl. the data-contract boundary — interpolated data values not renamed) as the `FRONTEND-CONVENTIONS.md` "Naming conventions" section. Zero render change verified by headless-Chrome byte-identical screenshots (7/8 pages; wizard.html normalized-DOM identical, its JSON export embeds `Date.now()`); all 45 JS modules `node --check`; canonical.html + plan-data.js untouched.

body: IA / visibility **initial envisioning** recorded (user think-out, 2026-05-23) → `docs/ia-visibility-envisioning.md`. The *behavior/visibility* companion to `FRONTEND-CONVENTIONS.md` (which fixes structure/naming). **Status: initial envisioning — design intent, NOT a binding spec; refinable; front-end direction still unchosen.** Resolving idea for "don't overwhelm divisions/positions, yet keep everything visible when & as needed": **default to relevance; full set one step away.** "when" = pull / in-view relevance ranking (NO push — notifications are an explicit exclusion); "as" = altitude-appropriate granularity. Three actor altitudes (Role retired): **Division** (unit), **Position** (seat via Holding), **Person**. Principles: progressive disclosure / drill-don't-dump; **exception-first** (blocked/overdue/stalled/overbooked/due-soon/newly-assigned); **time-horizon** (Now·Next·Later); altitude-appropriate density (head=signals, holder=seat tasks, person=detail); **one consolidated home per person, faceted by altitude** (US-ORG-15/16); pull-not-push. Per-altitude: Division head = roll-up + exceptions, drill the `parent` tree (US-ORG-12/19; `d-dashboard` prototype); Position = one worklist faceted by seat (needs `Assignment.position`); Person = consolidated my-work. Enablers ready: division roll-up, blocked (open `ActionStepImpediment`), fixed-date overdue/due-soon, stalled (transition-log `at`). Gated by gaps (`source-model-gaps.md` §I): clean Position scoping (`Assignment.position`), true overbooked (capacity), recurring items (no RRULE). Constraints baked: no notifications, no KPIs; step actions still write through services+constraints (DEC-20). Not built — design precedent the chosen front-end inherits.

body: Front-end **suite-selector minimalist envisioning** persisted (user; web-grounded agent exploration 2026-05-23) → `docs/suite-selector-envisioning.md`. The third front-end precedent doc, paired with `FRONTEND-CONVENTIONS.md` (structure) + `docs/ia-visibility-envisioning.md` (behavior). **Status: initial envisioning — not built; front-end direction unchosen.** Need: a dev-time harness to run **2–3 real Django template+static "suites"** over the SAME views/models with a runtime **selector**, compare, and finalize one. **Research verdict: canonical Django pattern, NO dependency** — every theming package is stale or off-target; frameworks reduce to the same shape. The pattern (~30–40 lines, zero deps): active suite in `request.session` (a tiny `/suite/<name>/` switcher view + `FRONTEND_SUITES` allowlist) → context processor exposes `{{ suite }}` → `get_template_names()` on the CBVs (a shared `SuiteTemplateMixin`) returns `f"{suite}/{template_name}"` (suite **in the template name** keeps the cached loader correct — the trap that sinks custom loaders) → `static/<suite>/…` namespacing via `{% static suite|add:'…' %}` → a `<select>` in the base. Maps 1:1 onto the repo: a suite = a dir, so `mockups/<x>/{css,js}` move mechanically to `templates/<suite>/` + `static/<suite>/`; same views feed all suites (data-contract boundary per FRONTEND-CONVENTIONS). Settings deltas: `TEMPLATES[0]["DIRS"]=[BASE_DIR/"templates"]` (was `[]`; `APP_DIRS` stays) + add `STATICFILES_DIRS`. Collapses to a plain single-suite app at finalization (move winner up, delete others + the selector). Flags: ES-module sibling imports stay relative (only the entry goes through `{% static %}` — preserve per-suite js layout); `plan-data.js` stays a shared classic script **outside** suite dirs (`static/shared/…`) so all suites read one `window.DATA`; `canonical.html` sits **outside** the switcher (output-frozen). Not built — **next: user picks which 2–3 mockups become candidate suites**, then build = own plan→impl.

Sequencing (confirmed, per `docs/suite-selector-envisioning.md`): build the first suite's **plan-creation view (e-wizard) as real Django FIRST** — views/forms/templates/static in the suite-namespaced layout `templates/wizard/` + `static/wizard/`, a plain URL, a hardcoded suite prefix; **defer the switcher mechanism** until a 2nd comparable surface exists (it's a drop-in then); adopt the suite-namespaced **layout** from day one so no later refactor. body: Plan-creation front-end architecture DECIDED (building). New **`planner`** app: a **SHARED canonical Django form layer** (ModelForms + nested `inlineformset_factory` formsets + per-kind `clean()` mirroring the model CheckConstraints + school-scoped FK querysets) + a **shared single-page create view**; a **"suite" = presentation skin only** (Django template inheritance + per-suite static), first suite **`wizard`** skinned from `mockups/e-wizard` (the recomposability heuristic: forms decided once, NOT per suite). **Multi-step mechanism = Option C (user choice):** one server-rendered page holding all forms/formsets; the e-suite ES-module JS paginates client-side (progressive enhancement — works without JS); **one atomic POST** creates the Plan graph (`author`/`school` stamped, `lifecycle=proposed`). Minimal but extensible (a `steps.py` registry + shared partials + template blocks → adding steps/fields/suites/promote/draft-resume is additive, not a refactor). `django-formtools` REJECTED (can't model the nested action-step formsets; commits-at-end vs durable draft). **Capture = plain ORM, zero `services.py` calls** (a draft Plan saves with only school/title/cycle/author; the promotion gate runs only at advance-to-Active). Scope first cut: full e-step capture → **save Proposed** (US-1..21). Resolves **US-UI-1** (structured `Timeline` capture replacing e's `timeline_text`). Deferred (additive): promote-to-Active (US-22 via `advance_plan_status`), durable mid-way draft-save/resume, the runtime suite-selector (DEC-24), `ActionStepDependency` capture, Position-as-responsible (§I `Assignment.position`), Evidence payload (§H.1). App-owned `templates/<suite>/`+`static/<suite>/` (APP_DIRS, zero settings churn); suite prefix hardcoded `wizard/` (selector deferred per DEC-26). Plan: `~/.claude/plans/gleaming-skipping-emerson.md`. **IMPLEMENTED `1f5c815`** (the `planner` app — 31 files): `forms.py` (canonical ModelForms + per-kind `clean()` mirrors + school-scoped FK querysets), `formsets.py` (nested `inlineformset_factory` + `save_nested`), `steps.py` (registry), `views.py` (`PlanCreateView` one-page/one-atomic-POST + `DraftListView`/`DraftDetailView`), shared `templates/planner/*` + `wizard` suite skin (`templates/wizard/*` + `static/wizard/{css,js}` ported from e-wizard; JS = progressive enhancement only, page works JS-off), tests (forms-clean / create-view / happy-path). Registered `planner` in INSTALLED_APPS + URL include in `i18n_patterns`; no models/migrations. Gate green: check 0, makemigrations clean, ruff planner-clean (+format), **mypy 235**, **pytest 720** (+34). Capture = plain ORM, no services; stamps author/school/lifecycle=proposed. **US-UI-1 → enabled** (structured `TimelineForm` kind+dates). **Implementation note (within plan):** intra-graph references created in the same POST (MeasurementChannel→FeedbackChannel, ReviewEventInput→FeedbackChannel, RevisionRule.trigger→ReviewEvent) are captured by create-order **index** and resolved server-side after the referenced rows save (no pk exists on a fresh create) — fine for create; when **edit/draft-resume** lands it must switch to pk refs. Human browser smoke (create a plan via `manage.py runserver`) is the remaining check, available now.

body: LLM author-assist **initial envisioning** recorded (user-commissioned agent think-out, 2026-05-25) → `docs/llm-assist-envisioning.md`. The "where + how does the model assist a human author create a draft plan" companion to DEC-21 (the built `ai` app) and the front-end precedents. **Status: initial envisioning — design intent for review, NOT a binding spec, NOT built, no path chosen.** Codebase-grounded (at `be40e59`): maps every assist onto the real `planner/steps.py` seven steps (`basics→criteria→feedback→milestones→phases→steps→review`), the real `forms.py` `clean()` backstops, the `formsets.py` create-order **index** reference discipline, and the `ai` surface (`PromptTemplate.render()`→sanitizer, `LLMCall`, `get_llm_logged().stream()`). ~16 assists across the flow (A1 narrative prose + A1b zh-hans, A2 WASC-standard alignment, A3 domain M2M selection, A4 priority-deviation rationale, B1 typed success criteria + B2 channel binding, C1 feedback channels, D1 milestones+improvement-type+method, E1 phases, F1 method-recipe→action-steps [flagship, uses seeded `PlanningStep.template`], F2 assignments, F3 structured timeline, F4 resources+substeps, F5 evidence [no re-entry path yet], G1 whole review-loop, H1 read-only coherence critique, H2 orchestrated whole-draft). Each named with input context, a concrete `ai.PromptTemplate` (slug + variables + a strict JSON OUTPUT CONTRACT carried in the template text), the exact model fields it proposes, and its validated re-entry (DEC-20: model is the contract — AI proposes editable prefills → human accepts/edits → ONE atomic POST → every `clean()`/CheckConstraint fires; no privileged write). Shared `ai/services/grounding.py::build_grounding(school, draft_state)` assembles school profile + seeded vocabularies + WASC corpus + draft serialization (+ optional prior-plan few-shot), per-assist sliced for token budget. Additive `ai`-model fields flagged (not assumed): nullable `LLMCall.school`/`plan` FK (DEC-21/§I deferred), optional `PromptTemplate` JSON-schema field. Gaps the assists lean on, named: `Assignment.position` (§I), empty `Person` roster (D7), no RRULE (§I), no resource quantity/unit (§I), `ActionStepDependency`/`Evidence` not in the wizard (DEC-27/`steps.py`), off-request streaming infra not built (DEC-20/21). Exclusions honored: no KPIs (quantification only via target-kind `SuccessCriterion`), no notifications, no hooks, no US augmentation. Not built — design precedent for review; user next decides which assists (if any) to build, then own plan→impl.

Gaps the assists lean on, named: `Assignment.position` (§I), empty `Person` roster (D7), no RRULE (§I), no resource quantity/unit (§I), `ActionStepDependency`/`Evidence` not in the wizard (DEC-27/`steps.py`), off-request streaming infra not built (DEC-20/21). Exclusions honored: no KPIs (quantification only via target-kind `SuccessCriterion`), no notifications, no hooks, no US augmentation. Not built — design precedent for review; user next decides which assists (if any) to build, then own plan→impl.

title: North-star: AI-orchestrated, promotion-ready complete draft
body: (user think-out + read-only agent coverage analysis, 2026-05-25). Target end-state: a **school-admin** user (create-plan perms) writes ONLY **current state + desired end state**; the **`llm-user`** then sequences a dependency-aware heuristic and proposes **ALL** remaining `Plan`-graph elements (framing/priority/standards/alignment; milestones+improvement_type+method; phases; action steps + assignments/timeline/resources/evidence; success criteria + measurement; feedback channels; communications; review events; revision rules); the result is a **fully completed DRAFT** (`lifecycle=proposed`) captured through the model-is-contract path (DEC-20 — editable prefills, every `clean()`/CheckConstraint backstops; the AI never persists or flips); the **school admin ALONE** flips draft→active. **Coverage analysis verdict:** the spine exists (DEC-27 create path; US-LLM-1..5 seam enabled; grounding; A1/US-LLM-6 enabled; US-22 promote service gated to `school_admin`/DISC-18), but the bulk is unbuilt AND one recorded artifact was **defective** — **US-LLM-23/H2's recorded sequence (`A1→A2→A3→D1→F1→B1→C1→G1`) is provably partial**: it omits E1/F2/F3/F4/F5/A4, all gate-required per `plans/services.py::_check_promotion_gates`, so a draft from it would FAIL its own promotion gate. **Four confirmed forks:** (1) the orchestrated draft must be **promotion-READY** (passes the gate) → requires a pre-submit gap-closure pass; (2) record the missing pieces as **new atomic stories US-LLM-24** (the explicit dependency-aware sequencing heuristic over ALL gate-required elements) **+ US-LLM-25** (the pre-submit coherence/gap-closure pass re-invoking per-element assists until every gate predicate holds), with **US-LLM-23 corrected** to the orchestration that runs 24 then 25 → promotion-ready, input = the admin's current+desired state (framing/priority/type now AI-proposed); (3) the admin-alone flip must live **on the plan surface** → new **US-UI-6** (admin-only promote draft→active control calling `advance_plan_status`, gated `change_plan` + own-school; DEC-27 had deferred it); (4) **division-only responsibility is an acceptable complete draft** (gate-valid, US-ORG-6) — named-person assignment (D7 roster / §I `Assignment.position`) is a later enhancement, NOT on the critical path. **Minimal closure set (hard blockers, each its own plan→impl):** US-UI-3 (evidence — gate-required) · US-UI-5 (milestone/phase structure) · the per-element assists **US-LLM-7..21** · **US-LLM-24** (heuristic) · **US-LLM-25** (gap-closure) · **US-LLM-23** (orchestration) · **US-UI-6** (admin promote). Soft/excluded (NOT in the set): US-UI-4 dependencies, D7 Person roster, §I `Assignment.position`, RRULE, Evidence artifact payload (§H.1), KPIs/notifications/hooks. Persisted: stories in `phases/00-preamble.md` (US-LLM-23 corrected + US-LLM-24/25 + US-UI-6) + `phases/US-STATUS.md` (pending); `docs/llm-assist-envisioning.md` H2 annotated with the correction. Not built — DEC-29 + the analysis define the path. **Crystallized thesis (user, 2026-05-26 — the one-line statement of this north-star, fully grounded by DEC-32/DISC-22):** *"Given your vision, given the WASC criteria, given divisions and positions and their stated responsibilities (which also functions as a job description), given your school-wide learning outcomes, and given your stakeholders — what is the best route (using deep knowledge of learning and pastoral and teaching etc. best practices) through the modeled framework to the desired state, through a series of steps and milestones."* Each given has a model home (vision/mission → `GuidingStatement`+clauses; WASC criteria → `AccreditationStandard`+`PlanAccreditationStandard.rationale`; divisions+positions+responsibilities[=job descriptions] → `DivisionResponsibility`+`PositionResponsibility` [DISC-22]; SLWOs → `LearnerOutcome`; stakeholders → `StakeholderGroup`); the best-practice knowledge is the `llm-user`'s own reasoning; the route = steps+milestones over the Plan graph via the staged orchestration (US-LLM-23/24/25). The responsibility atoms double as the role's standing **job description** (single source of truth — both plan-allocation grounding AND the JD the role is accountable to). **The route ALSO encodes two WASC loops the one-liner under-states (both already modeled + GATE-ENFORCED by `_check_promotion_gates`, so a promotion-ready draft cannot omit them):** (a) **incorporating stakeholder feedback** — in FORMULATION via DEC-30 draft-review commenting (+ the DEC-33 per-stage feedback loop), and GOING-FORWARD via the operational `FeedbackChannel`(per stakeholder)/`Communication`/`ReviewEvent`-inputs (gate: ≥1 communication + ≥1 review event with ≥1 feedback-channel input; `services.py:182–189`); (b) **measuring progress with evidence + re-evaluating success** — `SuccessCriterion`(typed verification + target) with a `Measurement` bound to a feedback channel (gate `:135–145`), `Evidence` per step (gate `:162`), and `ReviewEvent`+`RevisionRule` as the re-evaluation process (gate `:184–190`), over `Milestone` checkpoints. So "the best route to the desired state" = steps+milestones THAT carry the feedback-incorporation + measurement/evidence/re-evaluation apparatus, not just task sequencing.

title: Draft review: stakeholder commenting on a proposed plan
body: (user think-out, 2026-05-25). New capability: during the **draft phase** (`lifecycle=proposed`), a set of school users — **routed by the plan's type/recipe** — may **comment on the draft itself**, as feedback to inform the school admin before they flip it to active (US-UI-6, DEC-29). **Terminology pinned:** "type of plan" = `ImprovementType`, "recipe" = `PlanningMethod` (both live per-`Milestone`, DEC-14). **Distinct from** the plan's operational `FeedbackChannel`/`Communication`/`ReviewEvent` (execution-time mechanisms the plan establishes) — this is a review/comment layer over the draft. **Four confirmed forks (AskUserQuestion):** (1) **routing = a configured mapping** ImprovementType/PlanningMethod → reviewer audience; a draft's commenters = the **union across its milestones'** type/recipe rules (NOT per-draft selection, NOT a new plan-level kind — DEC-14's per-milestone type stands); (2) **reviewer identity = org structure** (`Position`/`Division` — commenters are holders of mapped Positions / members of mapped Divisions, via `Holding`/`Person.user`); (3) **comment scope = plan + elements** (a comment targets the whole plan OR a specific child: action step / milestone / success criterion / etc.); (4) **advisory only** — comments do NOT gate the flip; the admin alone still flips freely (DEC-29 unchanged); no required acknowledgment/resolution (acknowledgments are an exclusion). **New models needed (own plan→impl, not built):** `DraftComment` (FK `Plan` + a generic target [plan or child], author, body, created-at; allowed while proposed) and `DraftReviewerRule` (ImprovementType/PlanningMethod → Position/Division audience). **Stories (atomic, `user`-actor only — no `llm-user`):** US-REV-1 (admin configures the type/recipe→audience rule), US-REV-2 (a reviewer comments, plan- or element-level), US-REV-3 (author/admin sees the comments while reviewing the draft; advisory, non-gating), US-REV-4 (reviewer discovers drafts-to-review via a pull view). **Dependency:** live commenting needs the `Person` roster + `User` links (**D7**) — the mapping + models can be built/tested independently, but actual commenters require accounts. **Exclusions held:** no notifications (pull discovery, DEC-23 IA), no acknowledgment/resolution workflow (advisory), no KPIs, no hooks. **Model-is-contract:** comments are human-authored content (no `llm-user`); the admin remains the sole lifecycle gate. **OPEN sub-question (to think out further, recorded 2026-05-25):** fork (3) settled that comments target *plan + elements*, but the **atomicity/granularity** of the element case is unresolved — per `ActionStep` only? per *any* element (milestone / phase / success criterion / feedback channel / …)? which element types are comment-targets, and at what depth? The `DraftComment` generic-target shape (which models it may point at) settles only when this is thought out; until then "plan + elements" is directional, not final. Persisted: this DEC + `phases/00-preamble.md` "Draft review — stakeholder commenting (US-REV)" section + `phases/US-STATUS.md` (US-REV-1..4 pending). Not built; `domain-model.md` entries land when the models are built.

title: Client apply-layer behavioral test gate
body: (user-directed "full pipeline treatment", 2026-05-25; built `d30cd59`). The 10 AI author-assists each apply an LLM proposal CLIENT-side into the wizard's Django formsets (set fields, grow formsets via `addRows`'s `cloneNode`+reindex, navigate per-step / two-level-nested formsets via `stepChildFormset`). That layer was gated ONLY by `node --check` (syntax) — no behavioral coverage. The **B2 `data-prefix` navigation defect** passed `node --check` + all Python tests and was caught only by human reasoning during exploration: direct evidence the layer breaks in ways the prior gate cannot see. Per the project's "automate, don't human-pass-remind" discipline, this client behavior is code and belongs in the automated gate, not a deferred human browser pass. **Decision:** add a jsdom + Node-built-in `node --test` behavioral suite (`school-improvement-plans/jstests/`, 25 tests) asserting each apply/navigator function lands a prefill in the right rows (incl. a dedicated B2-regression), and fold it into the gate via a `make test-js` target (`node --check` all wizard modules + the suite). **Harness chosen by an independent, no-prejudice research agent** that empirically probed jsdom/happy-dom/linkedom on this machine's **Node v23.7.0** against the six exact behaviors, running the real `formsets.js` unmodified: **jsdom** wins (full fidelity incl. the decisive `cloneNode` of form controls + bubbling `change`); happy-dom runner-up; **linkedom disqualified** (`<select>.value` getter-only → throws on the code's `el.value=`); a zero-dep shim rejected (cannot faithfully do cloneNode + bubbling → false confidence); a headless browser disproportionate. **Pins/footprint:** `jsdom@27.0.1` (jsdom 28+/29 declare engines excluding odd Node 23, so npm resolves 27.0.1 here) — the project's FIRST npm footprint, dev/test-only; `package.json` at the `school-improvement-plans/` root carries `"type":"module"` (so Node imports the native-ESM wizard modules by relative path; blast-radius checked — every `.js` under the root is a wizard ESM module, no classic script regresses) + the single `jsdom` devDependency + a `test:js` script; `package-lock.json` committed (force-added past a user-global gitignore that ignores lockfiles); `node_modules/` gitignored. The **browser runtime is unchanged** (native `<script type="module">`, no bundler/transpile; jsdom never ships to the client). Nine `ai-assist.js` functions gained a test-only named `export` (logic unchanged; `initAiAssist` stays the boot entry). **Gate-doc updates:** `CLAUDE.md` workflow step 7, `phases/PHASE-LAUNCH-CHECKLIST.md` step 7, and `templates/impl-static.md` `<pre_commit_checks>` now list `make test-js` (with the `npm ci`-once precondition since deps are gitignored). **Surfaced a latent defect → DISC-21-0525-A** (`reindexRow` reindexes the row `name` but not the `id_`-prefixed `id`/`for` → duplicate ids on added rows; recorded, not fixed). **Noted follow-up (NOT a task):** moving the test toolchain to Node 22 LTS / 24 would unlock jsdom 29.x. Verified: `make test-js` 25/25 + full Python gate green (mypy 260, pytest 1079).

title: Guiding-principle atoms + stored principle-linkages carrying rationale (the accreditation-evidence model), enriching the DEC-29 orchestration grounding
body: (user think-out, 2026-05-26; design SETTLED for the atom/linkage core, recorded on user direction after iterative validation; NOT built). **Frame:** a plan is a structured transformation of **current → desired state, measured by success criteria**, in which **each division, bounded by its purview/responsibilities, fosters purview-appropriate change**. The LLM reasons over structured GROUNDING ATOMS to decompose this rationally; the resulting plan-element↔atom linkages, **each carrying a `rationale`**, are precisely the evidence a WASC committee evaluates (and feed the `web/canonical.html` materialization, DEC-18) — so the rationale is a required output, not metadata.
continuation: **The atoms (structured grounding):**

1. **SLOs — resolved to the canonical 7 for the seed school:** `Engaged / Reflective / Compassionate / Confident / Innovative / Responsible / Bilingual Communicators`, each an existing `LearnerOutcome` row (model already carries `code`/`label`/`description`). A **(re)seed, NO schema change**, superseding the placeholder SLO seed. Verbatim description GIVEN for *Confident* ("Shows self-assurance in learning and communicating, knowing that growth happens through taking risks and expressing their ideas."); **the other 6 descriptions are canonical content OWED by the user (do NOT invent).** Already plan-linkable via A3's `Plan.learner_outcomes_targeted`.

2. **Vision + Mission statements — first-class atomicity** (NOT collapsed into the SLOs; the SLO/mission overlap is genre-inherent — "no universal definitions among schools" — and is left un-reconciled). A new per-school model holding the **verbatim prose** (kept for display + WASC materialization) decomposed into **ordered clause atoms** (the reasoning/matching units). Shape: a `kind` = vision | mission statement model + ordered clause children (text, order, **translated incl. `zh_hans` — first-class here**, the statement itself names "Chinese and English"). The **mission statement was given verbatim** (atomized: umbrella "thrive and lead in a modern global society" + stance "high expectations / guide + support" + 6 aim-clauses: engaged & realize potential; life-long learners leveraging the science of learning; ethic of personal responsibility; healthy self-esteem; communicate in Chinese & English; compassion for others). **The verbatim VISION statement is OWED by the user.**

3. **Division responsibilities — atomized:** a new `DivisionResponsibility` model (FK `division`, statement text [translated], `order`) = the per-division, particular responsibility atoms, **replacing the single 500-char `Division.scope_summary` blob as the mappable units** (scope_summary may remain as a human overview or be retired — open). Each atom is **single-division-owned** (no shared atoms); the "overlap" the user described is **concern-level co-recruitment** — a concern (e.g. *behavior*) recruits C&T (acts in lessons; evidence = observations/lesson-plans/PD) AND SAO (homeroom workshops; parent/student surveys), **each via its OWN atoms** — NOT atom-sharing.

**Reasoning frame (how the orchestration uses them):** orienting atoms "toward what" = vision clauses + mission clauses + SLOs; agency atoms "who/how" = each division's responsibility atoms (purview-bounded); delta "from→to, measured" = current → desired, by success criteria. The LLM matches each division's responsibilities against the orienting atoms over the delta — **drawing on its own learning + pastoral best-practice knowledge** — and drafts that division's purview-appropriate steps/evidence/feedback, **recording which atom each element advances/fulfills + the rationale**. Recruitment is **inferential** (no concern-tag lookup model — the model provides the structured atoms, the LLM supplies the reasoning). Output = editable prefill (model-is-contract, DEC-20).
**Stored principle-linkages, each with a `rationale` = accreditation evidence (the UNIFORM pattern, mirroring A2's `PlanAccreditationStandard`):**
- **WASC standards** → `PlanAccreditationStandard` (plan ↔ standard + rationale) — **EXISTS** (A2).
- **SLOs** → plan ↔ `LearnerOutcome` + rationale — **ENTAILED UPGRADE** of A3's plain `Plan.learner_outcomes_targeted` M2M to a through-model carrying rationale.
- **Mission-areas** → plan ↔ `MissionArea` + rationale — same entailed upgrade of A3's plain `mission_areas_targeted` M2M (pending the mission-areas-vocab vs mission-clauses relationship, below).
- **Vision + mission clauses** → plan ↔ clause + rationale — **NEW** plan-level through-model, parallel to standards.

- **Division responsibilities** → `ActionStepResponsibility` (step ↔ `DivisionResponsibility` + rationale; **M2M through-model** so a step may fulfill ≥1 responsibility, each with its own rationale, per the A2 precedent) — **NEW**.
The rationale across all five axes is the evaluable evidence trail and feeds the canonical WASC export (DEC-18).

title: Staged whole-draft orchestration + per-stage commentary + draft/stage PDF export
body: (user think-out, 2026-05-26; design captured on user direction; NOT built; builds on DEC-29 north-star, DEC-30 commenting, DEC-32 atoms/linkages, DEC-18 materialization). The DEC-29 orchestration is refined from a single auto-shot into a **staged, human-checkpointed build**, with commentary + PDF export hooked at the stage seams; the whole artifact stays `lifecycle=proposed` until the admin alone promotes (US-UI-6).
continuation: **(1) Staged orchestration.** US-LLM-24's heuristic runs as ordered STAGES, each a reasoning pass the LLM drafts → the human (admin/author) reviews + edits → explicitly **closes** ("responsibility breakdowns done, advance") → the next stage runs **grounded on the now-confirmed prior stages**. The admin is the decider at EVERY stage boundary (not just at the end) — model-is-contract (DEC-20) stage-by-stage. **Corrected pass order (division-responsibility BEFORE milestones):** (i) seed = current/desired/success-criteria; (ii) orienting-atom alignment (framing/priority + WASC standards + SLO + vision/mission clauses, each +rationale); (iii) **division-responsibility recruitment** — which divisions, per purview, bear on the change + which responsibility atoms are engaged (the "who + what spheres", which then SHAPES the milestones); (iv) milestones + improvement-type + method, organized over the recruited divisions' work; (v) per-division step decomposition (under phases/milestones) recording each step's fulfilled responsibility-atom + rationale (`ActionStepResponsibility`, DEC-32) + evidence + feedback; (vi) review loop (G1); (vii) gap-closure (US-LLM-25) → promotion-ready. **Reasoning order ≠ storage order** (no conflict): division-recruitment is an early reasoning pass whose OUTPUT shapes the milestones the LLM then proposes; milestones are still created before steps (steps need a phase/milestone parent); the `ActionStepResponsibility` rows are written at step-decomposition time. Roles: **US-LLM-23 = the staged runner** (drive pass N → checkpoint → advance); **US-LLM-24 = the stage order/heuristic** (with the corrected order); **US-LLM-25 = the final gap-closure stage**.

**(2) Stored drafting-stage sub-state (decision: option (b), stored — not UI-only).** The proposed plan carries a **stored, referenceable drafting-stage sub-state** (a stage enum / completed-stages record), **orthogonal to the `proposed`→`active` lifecycle** (it adds NO lifecycle states; proposed→active stays the single gate at US-UI-6). Chosen stored (over UI-session-only) because BOTH the stage-comment hook AND the stage-scoped export require the stage to be a referenceable entity — and because a long multi-pass, human-checkpointed build must be **resumable + auditable** (leave/return mid-build; another admin picks up; "responsibilities done, milestones pending" persists).

**(3) Per-stage commentary (extends DEC-30).** The stage becomes an additional **comment-target LEVEL** in `DraftComment`'s target set → **{plan, stage, element}** (the stage added). This is **additive and does NOT change/resolve DEC-30's open element-atomicity sub-question** (per-step? which element types? at what depth? — still open). It enables an optional **per-stage feedback loop**: at a stage checkpoint, before the admin closes it, the stage's drafted piece (a coherent unit) can be circulated for stakeholder comment (routed per DEC-30's ImprovementType/PlanningMethod→audience rules). The **build act** (author/admin constructing) and the **review act** (stakeholders commenting) stay distinct but MEET at the stage seam. Advisory only (DEC-30 unchanged) — comments never gate the admin's stage-close or final promotion.

**(4) Draft / stage PDF export (new — operationalizes DEC-18 for the draft phase).** A new capability the comment/distribution loop PRESUMES: an admin exports the **current draft plan — or a specific draft stage — as a PDF**, rendered via the **canonical (or a lighter draft-review) view template**, for distribution + commentary. This operationalizes DEC-18's materialization principle (model → canonical WASC shape) while the plan is still `proposed` — today canonical is a static prototype with no "render THIS plan → PDF" path. **Two scopes:** whole-draft + stage-scoped (the latter coherent only because stages are stored, (2)). **Template openness:** canonical = the heavy formal committee shape; a lighter review template is likely apter for stakeholder-comment distribution (canonical *or other* → probably both; the existing `DraftDetailView` at `/planner/drafts/<pk>/` is a plausible HTML basis for the review render). **Mechanism OPEN + deploy-adjacent:** the PDF approach (a Python renderer e.g. WeasyPrint, headless-chrome, or print-CSS) + which template — decide when scoped.

**Interlocks:** stored stages (2) enable both stage comments (3) and stage exports (4); the export (4) is the artifact the comment loop (3) circulates; the comment loop feeds the admin's promotion decision (US-UI-6). The whole staged build reasons over **DEC-32's atom/linkage grounding** (division responsibilities, vision/mission/SLO atoms, the rationale-bearing principle-linkages) — so DEC-32's reference + linkage layers are a **precondition** for a rational US-LLM-24, not a parallel track.

**Status:** design captured; **the user + llm-user stories it surfaces are listed for review THIS turn → to be particularized into `phases/00-preamble.md` + `phases/US-STATUS.md` after the user reviews the list** (NOT yet written there). Refinements pending to existing pending stories (US-LLM-23 → staged runner; US-LLM-24 → corrected pass order + stage definition; US-REV target set → add stage level + per-stage loop). New stories surfaced: stored drafting-stage state; admin stage-close/advance; per-stage feedback loop / stage-level comment (US-REV-5-ish); draft PDF export; draft-stage PDF export. Plus the DEC-32 dependency stories (atoms + rationale-linkages + the A3 SLO/mission-area rationale upgrade). Open mechanisms: PDF renderer/template; stored-stage shape; the DEC-30 element-atomicity sub-question (unchanged). Content still OWED by user (DEC-32): verbatim vision statement + 6 SLO descriptions. Exclusions hold: comments advisory/non-gating; no push notifications (pull-surface flags); no acknowledgments/hooks/KPIs.

title: Cross-plan coherence: draft-time overlap + timing/workflow-conflict detection
body: (user think-out, 2026-05-26; first raised inside the DEC-32 side-consideration, **promoted here to a first-class direction** so it is durably trackable rather than buried in a DEC paragraph; design NOT yet thought out; NOT built). At draft time, **detect a new plan's overlaps + timing/workflow conflicts against the school's EXISTING active plans, per division** — e.g. two plans both loading SAO's behavior-survey capacity in the same window, or duplicate/競合 action steps + timelines + division-purview load across plans — and **surface them on the draft-review surface for resolution** before the admin promotes. Operates at the **concern + timing + division-load** level (not a shared-atom level). **"Notification" = a pull-surface flag** (DEC-23 pull IA + DEC-30 draft-review surface), NOT push — unless the user later elects push (a posture change). **Beyond** DEC-29 (single-plan orchestration) and DEC-30 (draft commenting): this is cross-plan analysis over the school's plan set. **Needs its own think-out** — the detection model (what counts as an overlap/conflict; how division-load + timing windows are compared across plans), where it runs (a service over active plans, surfaced at draft review / per stage), and the user + llm-user stories — to be scoped before any build. **Actors when storied:** `user` (admin/reviewer sees + resolves the surfaced conflicts) + `llm-user` (the detection/explanation pass). Exclusions: pull-only (no push), advisory (does not gate promotion), no KPIs/hooks. Status: direction recorded; not designed; not built; awaiting the user's think-out + story scoping.

title: A Head of Department (HOD) is modeled as a `Position` under the subject-department `Division`, complementary to `Division.head`
body: (user decision, 2026-05-26; resolves the head-vs-position question surfaced after DISC-22; NO model change; a seed + admin-authored content follow). The user established: *"HOD responsibilities are meant to be part of the allocatable inventory the orchestration reasons over (US-LLM-24/27); the HOD needs a Position representation under the subject-dept division, not only the head FK."* **Why the head FK alone is insufficient (the resolved tension):** `Division.head` (FK→`Person`, SET_NULL) is a bare single-leader pointer for org rollup (`people_in_subtree`, `Person.heads`); it carries **no** `PositionResponsibility` atoms and `Assignment` has **no** "head" target (only `division`/`position`, DISC-22). So an HOD modeled *only* as `head` cannot carry role-specific responsibility atoms, cannot enter the DISC-22 division+position responsibility **inventory** the grounding emits, and cannot be the responsible party on an action step at the HOD grain. **The decision:** represent the HOD as a `Position` (a "Head of Department"-kind seat) under each subject-department `Division` (the children of Curriculum & Teaching) — a seat that carries `PositionResponsibility` atoms (its responsibilities, which double as its job description, DEC-29) and is `Assignment.position`-assignable, so US-LLM-24/27 allocate it. **Complementary, not redundant** (the user's "not only the head FK"): `Division.head` is RETAINED as the org-leader pointer (rollup, `Person.heads`, `people_in_subtree`); the HOD `Position` is the responsibility-bearing, plan-assignable seat. A person who leads a subject department is linked **both** ways — `Division.head` (the leader pointer) AND a `Holding` of the HOD `Position` (the seat). **NO model change** — `Position` + `PositionResponsibility` + `Assignment.position` + the grounding `_divisions` positions-nesting all exist (DISC-22, `a38c561`); the grounding inventory will include the HOD automatically once the HOD `Position` rows + their atoms exist as data. **Supersedes** the prior-art assertion in `docs/domain-model.md` (the `Position` entry's *"An HOD is not a position — it is the head"*), corrected this turn. **Scope (narrow):** this decision is about the **HOD / subject-department** case the user named; the same mechanism (a leader `Position` carrying responsibilities under any `Division`) is already supported by DISC-22 for any other division head, but whether other heads also get responsibility-bearing positions is the user's call when it arises — not prescribed here. **Follow-on build (own plan→impl; orchestrator-direct seed):** a data migration creating an HOD `Position` under each of the 5 seeded subject departments (Business and Economics, English, Humanities, Math, Sciences), keyed `(division, label)` per the `get_or_create` seed convention; its `PositionResponsibility` atoms are **admin-authored content (owed by user)**, like the other responsibility atoms. **Seed details to settle at seed time** (content, not structure): the seat label convention (a uniform "Head of Department" label per subject dept vs per-subject labels like "Head of English") and whether the HOD responsibility atoms are authored once as a shared role description or per-department (DISC-22's "responsibilities don't stay the same across positions" applies at the seat grain; the HOD *role* responsibilities are largely common across subjects with subject-specific content). Linking actual people (the `head` FK + the HOD `Holding`) needs the `Person` roster — **D7-blocked**; the Position + atoms build/seed independently. Persisted: this DEC + `docs/domain-model.md` (`Position` entry corrected; `Division.head` semantics note) + `ORCHESTRATOR-LOG.md` + `ORCHESTRATOR-STATE.md`. Exclusions/invariants held: model-is-contract (DISC-22 constraint unchanged); no new US story minted (the mechanism + admin editing already exist). **Default ("goes-without-saying") HOD responsibility set + the materialization mechanism + the three open forks (HOD-recognition, default-text evolution, the grain split) are thought out and persisted in `docs/hod-default-responsibilities.md`** (user-directed 2026-05-26; that doc is the design source for DEC-35's follow-on materialization seed and proposes resolving the shared-vs-per-dept sub-question as "shared defaults, per-dept overridable" — pending user validation).

title: Generalize DEC-35: the "leader = `Position` + `Division.head`" pattern applies to ANY `Division` whose leadership responsibilities should enter the DISC-22 allocatable inventory, not only HOD / subject-dept
body: (user decision, 2026-05-26; addresses cleavage carry-forward item 2 in `docs/tenant-vs-generic-cleavage.md`; NO model change; per-Division seeding decisions remain per-tenant). **The discovered shape** (Explore of `data/may-27-2026-staff-names-crs.csv`, 61-row staff CSV): every leadership unit in the tenant data carries a leader title — *Director of AAO* (Junbin Jin), *Director of Student Affairs Office* (Chengli Chen), *Director of Teaching and Curriculum Center* (Steve Lu — effectively head of C&T), *IHS Principal* (Celestine Fang — head of PO), *Director of Future Scholar Innovation Center* (Axel Chen — unit-vs-program question still open), *College Counseling Department Coordinator* (Pengfei Liu), plus future positions added under any other Division — and each is structurally identical to the HOD case DEC-35 scoped narrowly: a "leader of a Division whose responsibilities should be allocatable in plans." DEC-35's narrow scope leaves these as ad-hoc decisions per-leadership-unit, which the data shows is unwarranted: the SAME mechanism applies uniformly. **The decision (the generalization):** for **ANY** `Division` whose leadership has allocatable-in-plans responsibilities, model the leader as a **`Position` under that `Division`** (labeled per tenant convention — "Director of X" / "Head of Department" / "Coordinator" / "Principal" / etc.), carrying `PositionResponsibility` atoms (the leader's responsibilities = its standing job description, DEC-29), and `Assignment.position`-assignable so US-LLM-24/27 allocate it. `Division.head` (FK→`Person`) stays as the **complementary** org-leader pointer for rollup (`people_in_subtree`, `Person.heads`); a real leader is linked **both** ways — the `head` FK AND a `Holding` of the leader `Position`. DEC-35 stays valid as the FIRST specific instance of the now-general pattern (HOD/subject-dept); DEC-36 just promotes the pattern from "HOD-only" to **"any leadership unit."** **NO model / migration / schema change** — DISC-22's machinery (`Position`, `PositionResponsibility`, `Assignment.position`, the grounding `_divisions` positions-nesting) already supports this for any `Division`. The generalization is a stated convention + a clear path so the roster-import seed doesn't have to invent per-unit treatments. **Consequence for the Chiway tenant's roster seed** (intake doc `data/school-content-intake-2026-05-26.md` §5 / §7 / §8): the HOD positions under the 5 subject-depts (DEC-35) PLUS leader `Position` rows under each non-subject Division that needs one (AAO/SAO/PO/C&T/Recruitment & Admissions/College Counseling/Library/Art/Music & Sports, with the Future Scholar Innovation Center carrying its own open unit-vs-program question). Each tenant decides which Divisions need a leader-`Position` seeded vs. just a `head` FK — the convention is "if the leader's responsibilities should be allocatable in plans, model them as a `Position`." **Open question DEC-36 inherits unresolved (from `docs/hod-default-responsibilities.md` fork 1):** how the system **recognizes** a leader `Position` versus other positions on a Division (label convention / structural via `head` / a role-type marker). DEC-35's seed and DEC-36's broader seed both need this settled before they land; the answer is the same for both (settling it once unblocks all leader-`Position` seeds). **Exclusions / invariants held:** model-is-contract (DISC-22 constraint unchanged); no new US story minted (mechanism + admin editing already exist; the existing US-UI-11 — admin authors division-responsibility atoms — and admin per-Position editing cover both grains uniformly); no editing of historical DEC-35 (DEC-35 stays as-recorded; DEC-36 extends it). Persisted: this DEC + `docs/tenant-vs-generic-cleavage.md` (item 2 marked addressed; cleavage-bleed point 3 updated) + `docs/domain-model.md` (`Division.head` + `Position` entries cross-ref both DEC-35 and DEC-36) + `ORCHESTRATOR-LOG.md` + `ORCHESTRATOR-STATE.md`. **Cleavage carry-forward item 2 → ADDRESSED.**

title: The HOD/leader-recognition fork resolves to `Position.kind` (a `TextChoices` field on `Position` with values `STAFF` (default) and `LEADER`)
body: (user decision, 2026-05-27; built `dbe1685`; settles fork 1 of `docs/hod-default-responsibilities.md`; unblocks DEC-35 HOD seeds + DEC-36 Director-of-X seeds + the year-leader Position splits). **The discovered need:** three downstream seed items (DEC-35 HOD positions per subject dept; DEC-36 Director-of-X positions per leadership unit; year-leader Position splits per `YearGroup`) all required a structural way to identify a leadership `Position` vs an ordinary staff `Position`. The three candidate paths were (a) label-substring convention, (b) structural inference via `Division.head`, and (c) an explicit role-type marker on `Position`. Paths (a) and (b) are fragile and ambiguous respectively (mandate-004 ruled them out). Path (c) is the canonical answer. **The decision:** add `school.choices.PositionKind(TextChoices)` with `STAFF = "staff"` / `LEADER = "leader"`; add `Position.kind = CharField(max_length=16, choices=PositionKind.choices, default=PositionKind.STAFF)`. Default `STAFF` keeps the 6 existing seeded Positions correct; only future HOD/Director/Year-Leader seeds set `LEADER`. NO `CheckConstraint` (choices alone bind valid values — same posture as `ActionStepStatus`). `PositionAdmin` exposes `kind` in `list_display` + `list_filter`. Grounding `_divisions` emits `"kind"` per position so the LLM orchestration sees the staff-vs-leader distinction. **DEC-35's "no model change" framing is superseded** here — generalizing the pattern (via DEC-36 + this fork) implicitly accepts that structural support is needed; the cost (one field, one migration) is small. **One migration**: `school/0047_position_kind.py` (AddField with default; reversible). NO grant migration (perms are model-scoped, not field-scoped). **Gate re-run independent + dev-DB applied + verified**: all 6 existing positions default to `kind=staff`; round-trip `staff↔leader` works; grounding emits `"kind"` for each position with both values surfaceable. **Now unblocked**: the actual HOD seeds (DEC-35), Director-of-X seeds (DEC-36), and year-leader Position splits — each becomes its own plan→IMPL with `Position(..., kind=PositionKind.LEADER)` written cleanly. Persisted: DEC-37 + `docs/hod-default-responsibilities.md` fork 1 marked RESOLVED + LOG + STATE.

title: Plan continuation is typed, not boolean: extend `PlanPredecessor` with a `continuation_type` (`TextChoices`: `EXTENSION` / `REVISION` / `RENEWAL` / `SUPERSESSION`) + a translated `rationale` text field
body: (user decision, 2026-05-28; settled via the canonical-envisioning think-out in this turn's conversation; one model change + one migration). **The discovered need:** the consultant-doc plan-proposal list in `web/data/plan-data.js::nonModel.proposals` (lines 652-699) carries a boolean `continuation: true` flag on 4 of 14 proposals (`p-pd-eval-align`, `p-summer-pd-reading`, `p-plc-build`, `p-yungu-customize` indirectly — the explicit `continuation: true` rows are `p-pd-eval-align` and `p-plc-build`); the structural primitive `PlanPredecessor` (self-referential M2M through-model, asymmetric, `CheckConstraint(name=planpredecessor_no_self_loop)`, PROTECT on the predecessor side) exists and is correct, but carries only two FKs — no semantic about WHAT KIND of continuation, no rationale prose. WASC accreditation cares about plan trajectory, and the trajectory has visibly different real-world flavors: sustained focus on uncompleted work; reflection-driven re-do of completed work; cyclical recurring practice; adaptive supersession of approaches that didn't land. Without typing, every continuation looks identical to the system and to the WASC-canonical render; with typing, each carries different evidence semantics for a WASC reviewer at a glance. **The decision:** add to `plans.choices` (or a new continuation-specific choices home if the user later decides) a `ContinuationKind(TextChoices)` enum with EXACTLY four members (the user's stated set, no more, no less): `EXTENSION = "extension"` (predecessor wasn't completed; current plan picks up where it left off — evidence of sustained, multi-year focus), `REVISION = "revision"` (predecessor was completed but findings drove a re-do at higher fidelity — evidence of reflection-driven improvement; high-value for accreditation), `RENEWAL = "renewal"` (cyclical recurring work; same shape, new cycle — evidence of standing practice / institutional discipline), `SUPERSESSION = "supersession"` (prior approach didn't work; current plan replaces with a different approach toward the same goal — evidence of adaptive learning from failure). Add `PlanPredecessor.continuation_type = CharField(max_length=16, choices=ContinuationKind.choices)` (NOT-NULL, NO default — every predecessor edge MUST declare its kind; an untyped edge is the failure case the typing is here to prevent). Add `PlanPredecessor.rationale = TextField(blank=True)` registered with modeltranslation in `plans/translation.py` — translated, school-authored prose explaining what was learned / carried / changed, mirrors the DEC-32 uniform rationale-on-every-linkage pattern (`PlanAccreditationStandard.rationale`, `PlanGuidingClause.rationale`, `ActionStepResponsibility.rationale`, etc.). `rationale` allowed blank for backward-compat (the existing zero `PlanPredecessor` rows in the dev DB don't need a forced value), but the canonical render expects it for evidence-bearing continuation cases. NO `CheckConstraint` on `continuation_type` (the `TextChoices` enum binds valid values — same posture as `Lifecycle`, `ActionStepStatus`, `PositionKind`). NO behavior on `Plan` (no `Plan.is_continuation` field — that's a derived property in the future if/when needed; storing it would just drift from the truth on the `PlanPredecessor` rows). **Migration shape:** `plans/0042_planpredecessor_continuation_type_and_rationale.py` (or whatever the auto-gen names it): one `AddField` for `continuation_type` + three `AddField`s for `rationale` (`rationale`, `rationale_en`, `rationale_zh_hans` via modeltranslation; all `TextField(blank=True)`); dep `("plans", "0041_seed_proposed_plans")` or whatever the latest plans migration is at land time. Reversible by Django's auto-RemoveField. **The 0 existing `PlanPredecessor` rows on dev DB** (no Plans → no predecessor edges) means no data-fix migration is needed; the NOT-NULL `continuation_type` with no default is safe because no rows exist to be forced into a default value. (If a forced-default is needed for makemigrations to land cleanly, use `EXTENSION` as the default — most common case for genuine continuation — but prefer no-default if Django accepts the migration.) **Grant migration**: `plans/__N__` strips/adds nothing — `school_admin`'s `PlanPredecessor` perms already cover field-level changes (perms are model-scoped). **Admin**: `PlanPredecessorAdmin` (if one exists) gains `continuation_type` in `list_display` + `list_filter` and `rationale` in `search_fields` + the change-form; if no admin exists today, this DEC does not demand creating one (the linkage is admin-edited via the `Plan` change-form's inline if the existing pattern uses inlines; verify at IMPL time). **Grounding**: no immediate grounding-emitter change in scope of this DEC — the orchestration trio doesn't currently reason over plan-predecessor edges; if/when US-LLM-* assists need this, a separate plan→IMPL adds the `_plan_predecessors` emitter. **The canonical render contract** (`web/canonical.html` + `web/data/plan-data.js` consumed by it): when this lands, `canonical.html` is expected to surface a "Continuation of:" section near the top of the plan that lists each named predecessor with its `continuation_type` (display label, e.g. "Extension of:" / "Revision of:" / "Renewal of:" / "Supersession of:") and its `rationale` prose, linking to the predecessor plan's record. Whether `canonical.html` is updated in this DEC's IMPL or in a follow-on plan is the user's call — this DEC settles the MODEL, not necessarily the canonical-render update. **Out of scope of this DEC (mandate-007 surfaces, user decides):** (1) The SOFT-CONTINUATION case — proposals/plans that continue prior work that was never modeled as a Plan record. The consultant-doc `continuation: true` flag on `p-pd-eval-align` and `p-plc-build` cannot be expressed via `PlanPredecessor` because the predecessor Plans don't exist in the dev DB. A separate `Plan.continuation_narrative` translated TextField (or folding into the existing `Plan.provenance`) would close this gap; not landed here. The 4 affected proposals would seed with their `continuation` flag dropped (or copied into `provenance` text) until soft-continuation has a home. (2) A `Plan.is_continuation` derived property — useful for queries / templates if/when callers need a one-liner check; trivial to add later as a `@property` on the model (no migration). NOT in scope here. (3) Multi-predecessor synthesis UI/queries (a plan with 2+ predecessors); already structurally supported by the M2M, no UI work in scope here. **Exclusions / invariants held:** model-is-contract (`PlanPredecessor` shape extends additively; no rename, no drop); no new US story minted (the linkage already existed; this typing makes it semantically useful but doesn't create new user-facing behavior on its own); the `planpredecessor_no_self_loop` constraint stays untouched. **Cross-references:** the consultant-doc `continuation: true` flags in `web/data/plan-data.js:652-699` are the discovered-need source; `data/seed-round-plan.md::DEC-32` is the rationale-on-through-model precedent the `rationale` field mirrors; the canonical render contract gap is noted but its closure deferred to a follow-on plan if/when scoped. Persisted: this DEC + `docs/domain-model.md` (`PlanPredecessor` entry added or extended) + `ORCHESTRATOR-LOG.md` + `ORCHESTRATOR-STATE.md` after IMPL lands.
